name: comparation_through_debate
description: Evaluate a debate between player_a and player_b using predefined criteria and identify the winner.

player_a_sell_himself.description: player_a presents their strengths and how they meet the criteria. Max of 100 words.

player_b_sell_himself.description: player_b presents their strengths and how they meet the criteria. Max of 100 words.

player_a_arguments.description: player_a arguments for why they should be the winner compared to player_b. Max of 100 words.

player_b_counter.description: player_b counter arguments for why they should be the winner compared to player_a. Max of 100 words.

player_a_adherence_score.description: Score (1-5) of how well player_a meets the explicit requirements of the criteria.

player_b_adherence_score.description: Score (1-5) of how well player_b meets the explicit requirements of the criteria.

player_a_quality_score.description: Score (1-5) of player_a's overall quality and detail completeness relative to the criteria.

player_b_quality_score.description: Score (1-5) of player_b's overall quality and detail completeness relative to the criteria.

player_a_risk_avoidance_score.description: Score (1-5) of how well player_a avoids conflicts, trade-offs, or negative factors relative to the criteria (higher is better/safer).

player_b_risk_avoidance_score.description: Score (1-5) of how well player_b avoids conflicts, trade-offs, or negative factors relative to the criteria (higher is better/safer).

impartial_judge_winner_reasoning.description: The detailed reasoning about why the impartial judge chose the winner. Max of 100 words.

impartial_judge_winner.description: Who is the winner based on the impartial judge reasoning?
